title: lupinus latifolius subsp. longipes
intro: Lupinus latifolius subsp. longipes, also known as the long-beaked lupine, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Consuming the plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially aiding in weight management. The plant can be prepared as a herbal supplement, tea, or infusion, and is also used as a food source for wildlife, particularly for its seeds and foliage.
